pages.text: &6Early Prospecting&r teaches your settlement to read the rock — the rust of iron, the green of copper, the dull glint of tin — so hidden ores become visible. Ore Mining then puts &6miners&r to those deposits.

A miner does not destroy blocks. They chip at ore boulders, swapping each to its mined state and letting the vein regenerate over time, so a deposit is worked rather than spent. Assign a miner and mark the claim with a point-marker like a herder's pen.

Mined ore feeds the smelting chain: the bloomery turns iron and copper into ingots, and raw tin feeds the crucible toward bronze. Prospecting Quarry, a culture doctrine, even lets quarry crews recover the occasional raw ore from plain stone.
